description:
Alertmanager at /home/user/monitoring keeps failing on startup — `./alertmanager --config.file=alertmanager.yml` just dies immediately with some yaml error about line 42. Thing is, line 42 looks fine to me, it's just a receiver name. We've got a bunch of includes and template refs in there so maybe something's getting pulled in wrong? The prometheus rules in /home/user/monitoring/rules/ also reference alert routes that should exist.

Need it actually starting and staying up on port 9093, and the existing alert routes need to stay intact — we have PagerDuty and Slack integrations that ops will murder me over if they break.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/monitoring/ contains:
  - alertmanager (real Alertmanager 0.27.0 binary, executable)
  - alertmanager.yml (main config)
  - templates/slack.tmpl (Slack notification template)
  - templates/pagerduty.tmpl (PagerDuty notification template)
  - receivers.yml (meant to be referenced but NOT actually included properly)
- /home/user/monitoring/rules/ contains Prometheus alerting rules (for context, not used by Alertmanager directly)

- alertmanager.yml contents:
```yaml
global:
  resolve_timeout: 5m
  slack_api_url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/T00000000/B00000000/XXXXXXXX'

templates:
  - 'templates/*.tmpl'

route:
  receiver: 'default-receiver'
  group_by: ['alertname', 'cluster']
  group_wait: 30s
  group_interval: 5m
  repeat_interval: 4h
  routes:
    - match:
        severity: critical
      receiver: 'pagerduty-critical'
    - match:
        severity: warning
      receiver: 'slack-warnings'
    - match_re:
        service: ^(foo|bar)$
      receiver: 'team-backend'

receivers:
  - name: 'default-receiver'
    slack_configs:
      - channel: '#alerts-general'
        send_resolved: true

  - name: 'pagerduty-critical'
    pagerduty_configs:
      - service_key: 'fake-pagerduty-key-12345'
        severity: critical

  - name: 'slack-warnings'
    slack_configs:
      - channel: '#alerts-warnings'
        send_resolved: true

  - name: 'team-backend'
    slack_configs:
      - channel: '#team-backend-alerts'
        send_resolved:  true
        title: '{{ template "slack.title" . }}'
        text: '{{ template "slack.text" . }}'

  - name: 'db-team'
    slack_configs:
      - channel: '#db-alerts'
        	send_resolved: false
```

- The bug: Line 42 area has a YAML indentation error — there's a tab character mixed with spaces before `send_resolved: false` in the db-team receiver (shown as `\t` above). YAML parsers reject tabs, and the error message points to line 42 which is where the parser gives up, but the actual tab is on the line with send_resolved.

- templates/slack.tmpl exists and contains valid Go template definitions for slack.title and slack.text
- templates/pagerduty.tmpl exists and contains valid Go template definitions

- The agent user owns /home/user/monitoring and all contents (writable)
- No other processes running on port 9093

Expected final state:
- `cd /home/user/monitoring && ./alertmanager --config.file=alertmanager.yml &` starts successfully and stays running
- `curl -s http://localhost:9093/-/healthy` returns 200 with body containing "ok" within 10 seconds of startup
- `curl -s http://localhost:9093/api/v2/status` returns valid JSON with "cluster" field present

Invariants:
- All six receivers must remain defined: default-receiver, pagerduty-critical, slack-warnings, team-backend, db-team (can verify via `curl http://localhost:9093/api/v2/receivers` returning JSON array with 5+ items)
- The route tree structure must remain intact (critical→pagerduty, warning→slack, service regex→team-backend)
- templates/ directory and both .tmpl files unchanged
- PagerDuty service_key value unchanged
- Slack channel names unchanged

Anti-shortcut guards:
- Alertmanager must actually be running (process exists, port 9093 responding) — not just config file edited
- Cannot delete the db-team receiver to "fix" the error — all 5 custom receivers must exist in API response
- Cannot replace alertmanager.yml with a minimal stub — must have at least 5 receivers and 3 sub-routes
name: task_000000_ed38d9d7
